content.thinking:
The user wants a complete end-to-end picture of how Hugo renders site content. This is a deep code exploration task across multiple packages. Let me spawn an Explore agent to map out this subsystem carefully.

This is a large exploration task that would benefit from spawning multiple agents to explore different parts of the pipeline in parallel:
1. Page assembly and content tree (hugolib/)
2. Page bundles resolution
3. Shortcode handling
4. Goldmark markdown conversion
5. Template rendering to HTML output

Let me spawn agents to explore these different parts concurrently.

content.text: I'll spawn several parallel Explore agents to map the full rendering pipeline across its major phases.

input.description: Explore Hugo page assembly and build pipeline in hugolib
input.prompt:
Map the Hugo build pipeline in hugolib/ — specifically the sequence of function calls that assembles pages and drives the full site build. I need file paths and line numbers for every key function.

Focus on:
1. The entry point: HugoSites.Build() and what it calls
2. Site.process() and the content tree assembly
3. How pages are created from content files (readAndProcessContent, createPageFromFile, etc.)
4. How page bundles (leaf bundles, branch bundles) are assembled and their resources resolved
5. The content tree types: PageMap, contentTreeNodeCallback, etc.

Search thoroughly in hugolib/ — look at hugolib/hugo_sites_build.go, hugolib/site.go, hugolib/page_collections.go, hugolib/content_map*.go, hugolib/page*.go. Report every function in the call chain with file:line. Be very thorough.

input.description: Explore Hugo Goldmark markdown conversion pipeline
input.prompt:
Map the Hugo Goldmark markdown conversion pipeline with exact file paths and line numbers.

Focus on:
1. How markup.Convert() is called for a page's content
2. The Goldmark converter setup: markup/goldmark/convert.go — the Convert() method
3. The Goldmark parser/renderer extensions Hugo registers (highlights, render hooks, etc.)
4. Render hooks: how Hugo intercepts image/link/heading rendering via goldmark render hooks
5. The RenderContext and DocumentContext types
6. How the converted HTML is returned and stored on the page

Search in: markup/goldmark/, markup/converter.go or markup/markup.go, markup/highlight/. Report every function in order with file:line. Be very thorough.

97

99		siteStateInit siteState = iota
100		siteStateReady
101	)
102	
103	type Site struct {
104		state       siteState
105		conf        *allconfig.Config
106		language    *langs.Language
107		store       *hstore.Scratch
108		siteWrapped page.Site
109	
110		// The owning container.
111		h *HugoSites
112	
113		*deps.Deps
114		*siteLanguageVersionRole
115	
116		relatedDocsHandler *page.RelatedDocsHandler
117	
118		publisher          publisher.Publisher
119		frontmatterHandler pagemeta.FrontMatterHandler
120	}
121	
122	func (s Site) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) (*Site, error) {
123		s.siteLanguageVersionRole = s.siteLanguageVersionRole.cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role)
124		d, err := s.Deps.Clone(&s, s.Conf)
125		if err != nil {
126			return nil, err
127		}
128		s.Deps = d
129		ss := &s
130		ss.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(ss)
131		return ss, nil
132	}
133	
134	// For debugging purposes only.
135	func (s *Site) resolveDimensionNames() types.Strings3 {
136		return s.Conf.ConfiguredDimensions().ResolveNames(s.siteVector)
137	}
138	
139	type siteLanguageVersionRole struct {
140		siteVector        sitesmatrix.Vector
141		isDefaultLanguage bool
142	
143		roleInternal roles.RoleInternal
144		role         roles.Role
145	
146		versionInternal versions.VersionInternal
147		version         versions.Version
148	
149		pageMap *pageMap
150

input.description: Explore Hugo page template rendering and HTML output
input.prompt:
Map the Hugo page template rendering pipeline — from selecting the right template to writing the final HTML output — with exact file paths and line numbers.

Focus on:
1. How renderPages() / renderPage() is called per page
2. Template lookup: how the template for a page is resolved (layout, kind, type, format)
3. Template execution: tpl/tplimpl execution, the ExecuteWithContext function
4. How .Content is made available (the PageContent / workContent types)
5. How the output is written: the target path resolution and writing to the output filesystem
6. The output format types (OutputFormat, formats.Format)

Search in: hugolib/render*.go (renderPage, renderPages), tpl/tplimpl/template.go, resources/page/page_content.go or similar, output/ package. Report every function in order with file:line. Be very thorough.

150	
151		// Page navigation.
152		*pageFinder
153		siteRefLinker siteRefLinker
154	
155		// Shortcut to the home page. Note that this may be nil if
156		// home page, for some odd reason, is disabled.
157		home *pageState
158	
159		// The last modification date of this site.
160		lastmod time.Time
161	
162		// Lazily loaded site dependencies
163		init *siteInit
164	
165		// The output formats that we need to render this site in. This slice
166		// will be fixed once set.
167		// This will be the union of Site.Pages' outputFormats.
168		// This slice will be sorted.
169		renderFormats output.Formats
170	}
171	
172	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) *siteLanguageVersionRole {
173		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Version] = version
174		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Role] = role
175		s.home = nil
176		s.lastmod = time.Time{}
177		s.init = &siteInit{}
178		return &s
179	}
180	
181	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) Role() roles.Role {
182		return s.role
183	}
184	
185	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) Version() versions.Version {
186		return s.version
187	}
188	
189	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) isDefault() bool {
190		return s.isDefaultLanguage && s.roleInternal.Default && s.versionInternal.Default
191	}
192	
193	func (s *Site) Debug() {
194		fmt.Println("Debugging site", s.Lang(), "=>")
195		// fmt.Println(s.pageMap.testDump())
196	}
197	
198	// NewHugoSites creates HugoSites from the given config.
199	func NewHugoSites(cfg deps.DepsCfg) (*HugoSites, error) {
200		if !cfg.TestCfg.IsZero() && !cfg.IsIntegrationTest {
201			panic("DepsCfg.TestCfg must only be set in integration tests")
202		}
203	
204		conf := cfg.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig()
205		rolesSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Roles.Config.Sorted
206		versionsSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Versions.Config.Sorted
207	
208		var (
209			poolSizeKatex = 2
210			poolSizeWebP  = 1
211			poolSizeAvif  = 1
212		)
213		if n := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier(); n > 1 {
214			poolSizeKatex = min(n, 8)
215			poolSizeWebP = max(2, n/2)
216			poolSizeAvif = max(2, n/2)
217		}
218	
219		var logger loggers.Logger
220		if cfg.TestLogger != nil {
221			logger = cfg.TestLogger
222		} else {
223			var logHookLast func(e *logg.Entry) error
224			if cfg.Configs.Base.PanicOnWarning {
225				logHookLast = loggers.PanicOnWarningHook
226			}
227			if cfg.StdOut == nil {
228				cfg.StdOut = os.Stdout
229			}
230			if cfg.StdErr == nil {
231				cfg.StdErr = os.Stderr
232			}
233			if cfg.LogLevel == 0 {
234				cfg.LogLevel = logg.LevelWarn
235			}
236	
237			logOpts := loggers.Options{
238				Level:              cfg.LogLevel,
239				DistinctLevel:      logg.LevelWarn, // This will drop duplicate log warning and errors.
240				HandlerPost:        logHookLast,
241				StdOut:             cfg.StdOut,
242				StdErr:             cfg.StdErr,
243				StoreErrors:        conf.Watching(),
244				SuppressStatements: conf.IgnoredLogs(),
245			}
246			logger = loggers.New(logOpts)
247	
248		}
249	
250		memCache := dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: conf.Watching(), Log: logger})
251	
252		var h *HugoSites
253		onSignalRebuild := func(ids ...identity.Identity) {
254			// This channel is buffered, but make sure we do this in a non-blocking way.
255			if cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
256				go func() {
257					cfg.ChangesFromBuild <- ids
258				}()
259			}
260		}
261	
262		compilationCacheDir := filepath.Join(conf.Dirs().CacheDir, "_warpc")
263	
264		imageWasmMemory := 384 // 384 MiB (4096 MiB Max)
265		if m := cfg.TestCfg.WarpcMemory; m > 0 {
266			imageWasmMemory = m
267		}
268	
269		firstSiteDeps := &deps.Deps{
270			Fs:   cfg.Fs,
271			Log:  logger,
272			Conf: conf,
273			BuildState: &deps.BuildState{
274				OnSignalRebuild: onSignalRebuild,
275			},
276			Counters:            &deps.Counters{},
277			MemCache:            memCache,
278			TranslationProvider: i18n.NewTranslationProvider(),
279			WasmDispatchers: warpc.AllDispatchers(
280				// Katex options.
281				warpc.Options{
282					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
283	
284					PoolSize: poolSizeKatex,
285					Infof:    logger.InfoCommand("katex").Logf,
286					Warnf:    logger.WarnCommand("katex").Logf,
287				},
288				// WebP options.
289				warpc.Options{
290					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
291					PoolSize:            poolSizeWebP,
292					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
293					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("webp").Logf,
294					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("webp").Logf,
295				},
296				// Avif options.
297				warpc.Options{
298					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
299					PoolSize:            poolSizeAvif,
300					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
301					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("avif").Logf,
302					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("avif").Logf,
303				},
304			),
305		}
306	
307		if err := firstSiteDeps.Init(); err != nil {
308			return nil, err
309		}
310	
311		// Prevent leaking goroutines in tests.
312		if cfg.IsIntegrationTest && cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
313			firstSiteDeps.BuildClosers.Add(types.CloserFunc(func() error {
314				close(cfg.ChangesFromBuild)
315				return nil
316			}))
317		}
318	
319		batcherClient, err := esbuild.NewBatcherClient(firstSiteDeps)
320		if err != nil {
321			return nil, err
322		}
323		firstSiteDeps.JSBatcherClient = batcherClient
324	
325		confm := cfg.Configs
326		if err := confm.Validate(logger); err != nil {
327			return nil, err
328		}
329		var sites []*Site
330	
331		ns := &contentNodeShifter{
332			conf: conf,
333		}
334	
335		treeConfig := doctree.Config[contentNode]{
336			Shifter:        ns,
337			TransformerRaw: &contentNodeTransformerRaw{},

14	package pageparser
15	
16	import (
17		"bytes"
18		"errors"
19		"fmt"
20		"io"
21		"regexp"
22		"strings"
23	
24		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/parser/metadecoders"
25	)
26	
27	// Result holds the parse result.
28	type Result interface {
29		// Iterator returns a new Iterator positioned at the beginning of the parse tree.
30		Iterator() *Iterator
31		// Input returns the input to Parse.
32		Input() []byte
33	}
34	
35	var _ Result = (*pageLexer)(nil)
36	
37	// ParseBytes parses the page in b according to the given Config.
38	func ParseBytes(b []byte, cfg Config) (Items, error) {
39		startLexer := lexIntroSection
40		if cfg.NoFrontMatter {
41			startLexer = lexMainSection
42		}
43		l, err := parseBytes(b, cfg, startLexer)
44		if err != nil {
45			return nil, err
46		}
47		return l.items, l.err
48	}
49	
50	type ContentFrontMatter struct {
51		Content           []byte
52		FrontMatter       map[string]any
53		FrontMatterFormat metadecoders.Format
54	}
55	
56	// ParseFrontMatterAndContent is a convenience method to extract front matter
57	// and content from a content page.
58	func ParseFrontMatterAndContent(r io.Reader) (ContentFrontMatter, error) {
59		var cf ContentFrontMatter
60	
61		input, err := io.ReadAll(r)
62		if err != nil {
63			return cf, fmt.Errorf("failed to read page content: %w", err)
64		}
65	
66		psr, err := ParseBytes(input, Config{})
67		if err != nil {
68			return cf, err
69		}
70	
71		var frontMatterSource []byte
72	
73		iter := NewIterator(psr)
74	
75		walkFn := func(item Item) bool {
76			if frontMatterSource != nil {
77				// The rest is content.
78				cf.Content = input[item.low:]
79				// Done
80				return false
81			} else if item.IsFrontMatter() {
82				cf.FrontMatterFormat = FormatFromFrontMatterType(item.Type)
83				frontMatterSource = item.Val(input)
84			}
85			return true
86		}
87	
88		iter.PeekWalk(walkFn)
89	
90		cf.FrontMatter, err = metadecoders.Default.UnmarshalToMap(frontMatterSource, cf.FrontMatterFormat)
91		return cf, err
92	}
93	
94	func FormatFromFrontMatterType(typ ItemType) metadecoders.Format {
95		switch typ {
96		case TypeFrontMatterJSON:
97			return metadecoders.JSON
98		case TypeFrontMatterORG:
99			return metadecoders.ORG
100		case TypeFrontMatterTOML:

1346	func (s *HugoSites) processFiles(ctx context.Context, l logg.LevelLogger, buildConfig *BuildCfg, filenames ...pathChange) error {
1347		if s.Deps == nil {
1348			panic("nil deps on site")
1349		}
1350	
1351		sourceSpec := source.NewSourceSpec(s.PathSpec, buildConfig.ContentInclusionFilter, s.BaseFs.Content.Fs)
1352	
1353		// For inserts, we can pick an arbitrary pageMap.
1354		pageMap := s.Sites[0].pageMap
1355	
1356		c := newPagesCollector(ctx, s.h, sourceSpec, s.Log, l, pageMap, buildConfig, filenames)
1357	
1358		if err := c.Collect(); err != nil {
1359			return err
1360		}
1361	
1362		return nil
1363	}

93	
94	var _ page.Site = (*Site)(nil)
95	
96	type siteState int
97	
98	const (
99		siteStateInit siteState = iota
100		siteStateReady
101	)
102	
103	type Site struct {
104		state       siteState
105		conf        *allconfig.Config
106		language    *langs.Language
107		store       *hstore.Scratch
108		siteWrapped page.Site
109	
110		// The owning container.
111		h *HugoSites
112	
113		*deps.Deps
114		*siteLanguageVersionRole
115	
116		relatedDocsHandler *page.RelatedDocsHandler
117	
118		publisher          publisher.Publisher
119		frontmatterHandler pagemeta.FrontMatterHandler
120	}
121	
122	func (s Site) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) (*Site, error) {
123		s.siteLanguageVersionRole = s.siteLanguageVersionRole.cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role)
124		d, err := s.Deps.Clone(&s, s.Conf)
125		if err != nil {
126			return nil, err
127		}
128		s.Deps = d
129		ss := &s
130		ss.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(ss)
131		return ss, nil
132	}
133	
134	// For debugging purposes only.
135	func (s *Site) resolveDimensionNames() types.Strings3 {
136		return s.Conf.ConfiguredDimensions().ResolveNames(s.siteVector)
137	}
138	
139	type siteLanguageVersionRole struct {
140		siteVector        sitesmatrix.Vector
141		isDefaultLanguage bool
142	
143		roleInternal roles.RoleInternal
144		role         roles.Role
145	
146		versionInternal versions.VersionInternal
147		version         versions.Version
148	
149		pageMap *pageMap
150	
151		// Page navigation.
152		*pageFinder
153		siteRefLinker siteRefLinker
154	
155		// Shortcut to the home page. Note that this may be nil if
156		// home page, for some odd reason, is disabled.
157		home *pageState
158	
159		// The last modification date of this site.
160		lastmod time.Time
161	
162		// Lazily loaded site dependencies
163		init *siteInit
164	
165		// The output formats that we need to render this site in. This slice
166		// will be fixed once set.
167		// This will be the union of Site.Pages' outputFormats.
168		// This slice will be sorted.
169		renderFormats output.Formats
170	}
171	
172	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) cloneForVersionAndRole(version, role int) *siteLanguageVersionRole {
173		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Version] = version
174		s.siteVector[sitesmatrix.Role] = role
175		s.home = nil
176		s.lastmod = time.Time{}
177		s.init = &siteInit{}
178		return &s
179	}

183	}

186		return s.version
187	}
188	
189	func (s siteLanguageVersionRole) isDefault() bool {
190		return s.isDefaultLanguage && s.roleInternal.Default && s.versionInternal.Default
191	}

200		if !cfg.TestCfg.IsZero() && !cfg.IsIntegrationTest {
201			panic("DepsCfg.TestCfg must only be set in integration tests")
202		}
203	
204		conf := cfg.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig()
205		rolesSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Roles.Config.Sorted
206		versionsSorted := cfg.Configs.Base.Versions.Config.Sorted
207	
208		var (
209			poolSizeKatex = 2
210			poolSizeWebP  = 1
211			poolSizeAvif  = 1
212		)
213		if n := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier(); n > 1 {
214			poolSizeKatex = min(n, 8)
215			poolSizeWebP = max(2, n/2)
216			poolSizeAvif = max(2, n/2)
217		}
218	
219		var logger loggers.Logger
220		if cfg.TestLogger != nil {
221			logger = cfg.TestLogger
222		} else {
223			var logHookLast func(e *logg.Entry) error
224			if cfg.Configs.Base.PanicOnWarning {
225				logHookLast = loggers.PanicOnWarningHook
226			}
227			if cfg.StdOut == nil {
228				cfg.StdOut = os.Stdout
229			}
230			if cfg.StdErr == nil {
231				cfg.StdErr = os.Stderr
232			}
233			if cfg.LogLevel == 0 {
234				cfg.LogLevel = logg.LevelWarn
235			}
236	
237			logOpts := loggers.Options{
238				Level:              cfg.LogLevel,
239				DistinctLevel:      logg.LevelWarn, // This will drop duplicate log warning and errors.
240				HandlerPost:        logHookLast,
241				StdOut:             cfg.StdOut,
242				StdErr:             cfg.StdErr,
243				StoreErrors:        conf.Watching(),
244				SuppressStatements: conf.IgnoredLogs(),
245			}
246			logger = loggers.New(logOpts)
247	
248		}
249	
250		memCache := dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: conf.Watching(), Log: logger})
251	
252		var h *HugoSites
253		onSignalRebuild := func(ids ...identity.Identity) {
254			// This channel is buffered, but make sure we do this in a non-blocking way.
255			if cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
256				go func() {
257					cfg.ChangesFromBuild <- ids
258				}()
259			}
260		}
261	
262		compilationCacheDir := filepath.Join(conf.Dirs().CacheDir, "_warpc")
263	
264		imageWasmMemory := 384 // 384 MiB (4096 MiB Max)
265		if m := cfg.TestCfg.WarpcMemory; m > 0 {
266			imageWasmMemory = m
267		}
268	
269		firstSiteDeps := &deps.Deps{
270			Fs:   cfg.Fs,
271			Log:  logger,
272			Conf: conf,
273			BuildState: &deps.BuildState{
274				OnSignalRebuild: onSignalRebuild,
275			},
276			Counters:            &deps.Counters{},
277			MemCache:            memCache,
278			TranslationProvider: i18n.NewTranslationProvider(),
279			WasmDispatchers: warpc.AllDispatchers(
280				// Katex options.
281				warpc.Options{
282					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
283	
284					PoolSize: poolSizeKatex,
285					Infof:    logger.InfoCommand("katex").Logf,
286					Warnf:    logger.WarnCommand("katex").Logf,
287				},
288				// WebP options.
289				warpc.Options{
290					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
291					PoolSize:            poolSizeWebP,
292					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
293					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("webp").Logf,
294					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("webp").Logf,
295				},
296				// Avif options.
297				warpc.Options{
298					CompilationCacheDir: compilationCacheDir,
299					PoolSize:            poolSizeAvif,
300					Memory:              imageWasmMemory,
301					Infof:               logger.InfoCommand("avif").Logf,
302					Warnf:               logger.WarnCommand("avif").Logf,
303				},
304			),
305		}
306	
307		if err := firstSiteDeps.Init(); err != nil {
308			return nil, err
309		}
310	
311		// Prevent leaking goroutines in tests.
312		if cfg.IsIntegrationTest && cfg.ChangesFromBuild != nil {
313			firstSiteDeps.BuildClosers.Add(types.CloserFunc(func() error {
314				close(cfg.ChangesFromBuild)
315				return nil
316			}))
317		}
318	
319		batcherClient, err := esbuild.NewBatcherClient(firstSiteDeps)
320		if err != nil {
321			return nil, err
322		}
323		firstSiteDeps.JSBatcherClient = batcherClient
324	
325		confm := cfg.Configs
326		if err := confm.Validate(logger); err != nil {
327			return nil, err
328		}
329		var sites []*Site
330	
331		ns := &contentNodeShifter{
332			conf: conf,
333		}
334	
335		treeConfig := doctree.Config[contentNode]{
336			Shifter:        ns,
337			TransformerRaw: &contentNodeTransformerRaw{},
338		}
339	
340		dimensionLengths := sitesmatrix.Vector{len(confm.Languages), len(versionsSorted), len(rolesSorted)}
341	
342		pageTrees := &pageTrees{
343			treePages: doctree.New(
344				treeConfig,
345			),
346			treeResources: doctree.New(
347				treeConfig,
348			),
349			treeTaxonomyEntries:           doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*weightedContentNode](dimensionLengths),
350			treePagesFromTemplateAdapters: doctree.NewTreeShiftTree[*pagesfromdata.PagesFromTemplate](dimensionLengths),
351		}
352	
353		pageTrees.createMutableTrees()
354	
355		for i, confp := range confm.ConfigLangs() {
356			language := confp.Language().(*langs.Language)
357			if language.Disabled {
358				panic("cannot create site for disabled language: " + language.Lang)
359			}
360			k := language.Lang
361			conf := confm.LanguageConfigMap[k]
362			frontmatterHandler, err := pagemeta.NewFrontmatterHandler(firstSiteDeps.Log, conf.Frontmatter)
363			if err != nil {
364				return nil, err
365			}
366	
367			langs.SetParams(language, conf.Params)
368	
369			s := &Site{
370				conf:               conf,
371				language:           language,
372				frontmatterHandler: frontmatterHandler,
373				store:              hstore.NewScratch(),
374				siteLanguageVersionRole: &siteLanguageVersionRole{
375					isDefaultLanguage: language.IsDefault(),
376					siteVector:        sitesmatrix.Vector{i, 0, 0},
377				},
378			}
379	
380			s.siteWrapped = page.WrapSite(s)
381	
382			if i == 0 {
383				firstSiteDeps.Site = s
384				s.Deps = firstSiteDeps
385			} else {
386				d, err := firstSiteDeps.Clone(s, confp)
387				if err != nil {
388					return nil, err
389				}
390				s.Deps = d
391			}
392	
393			// Set up the main publishing chain.
394			pub, err := publisher.NewDestinationPublisher(
395				firstSiteDeps.ResourceSpec,
396				s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
397				s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
398			)
399			if err != nil {
400				return nil, err
401			}
402	
403			s.publisher = pub
404			s.relatedDocsHandler = page.NewRelatedDocsHandler(s.conf.Related)
405			// Site deps end.
406	
407			sites = append(sites, s)
408	
409		}
410	
411		if len(sites) == 0 {
412			return nil, errors.New("no sites to build")
413		}
414	
415		sitesVersionsRoles := make([][][]*Site, len(sites))
416		for i := 0; i < len(sites); i++ {
417			sitesVersionsRoles[i] = make([][]*Site, len(versionsSorted))
418			for j := range versionsSorted {
419				sitesVersionsRoles[i][j] = make([]*Site, len(rolesSorted))
420			}
421		}
422	
423		siteVersionRoles := map[types.Ints2][]roles.Role{}
424		siteRoleVersions := map[types.Ints2][]versions.Version{}
425		// i = site, j = version, k = role
426		for i, v1 := range sitesVersionsRoles {
427			for j, v2 := range v1 {
428				for k := range v2 {
429					var vrs *Site
430					if j == 0 && k == 0 {
431						vrs = sites[i]
432					} else {
433						prototype := sites[i]
434						vrs, err = prototype.cloneForVersionAndRole(j, k)
435						if err != nil {
436							return nil, err
437						}
438					}
439					vrs.roleInternal = rolesSorted[k]
440					vrs.role = roles.NewRole(rolesSorted[k])
441					vrs.versionInternal = versionsSorted[j]
442					vrs.version = versions.NewVersion(versionsSorted[j])
443					siteRoleVersions[types.Ints2{i, k}] = append(siteRoleVersions[types.Ints2{i, k}], vrs.version)
444					siteVersionRoles[types.Ints2{i, j}] = append(siteVersionRoles[types.Ints2{i, j}], vrs.role)
445					vrs.pageMap = newPageMap(vrs, memCache, pageTrees)
446					vrs.pageFinder = newPageFinder(vrs.pageMap)
447					vrs.siteRefLinker = newSiteRefLinker(vrs)
448					vrs.prepareInits()
449					v2[k] = vrs
450				}
451			}
452		}
453	
454		h, err = newHugoSites(cfg, firstSiteDeps, pageTrees, sitesVersionsRoles)
455		if err == nil && h == nil {
456			panic("hugo: newHugoSitesNew returned nil error and nil HugoSites")
457		}
458	
459		return h, err
460	}
461	
462	func newHugoSites(
463		cfg deps.DepsCfg,
464		d *deps.Deps,
465		pageTrees *pageTrees,
466		sitesVersionsRoles [][][]*Site,
467	) (*HugoSites, error) {
468		first := make([]*Site, len(sitesVersionsRoles))
469		for i, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
470			first[i] = v[0][0]
471		}
472	
473		sitesVersionsRolesMap := map[sitesmatrix.Vector]*Site{}
474		for _, v1 := range sitesVersionsRoles {
475			for _, v2 := range v1 {
476				for _, s := range v2 {
477					sitesVersionsRolesMap[s.siteVector] = s
478				}
479			}
480		}
481	
482		numWorkers := config.GetNumWorkerMultiplier()
483		numWorkersSite := min(numWorkers, len(sitesVersionsRolesMap))
484		workersSite := para.New(numWorkersSite)
485	
486		var sitesLanguages []*Site
487		for _, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
488			sitesLanguages = append(sitesLanguages, v[0][0])
489		}
490	
491		h := &HugoSites{
492			Sites:                 first,
493			sitesVersionsRoles:    sitesVersionsRoles,
494			sitesVersionsRolesMap: sitesVersionsRolesMap,
495			sitesLanguages:        sitesLanguages,
496			Deps:                  first[0].Deps,
497			Configs:               cfg.Configs,
498			workersSite:           workersSite,
499			numWorkersSites:       numWorkers,
500			numWorkers:            numWorkers,
501			pageTrees:             pageTrees,
502			cachePages: dynacache.GetOrCreatePartition[string,
503				page.Pages](d.MemCache, "/pags/all",
504				dynacache.OptionsPartition{Weight: 10, ClearWhen: dynacache.ClearOnRebuild},
505			),
506			cacheContentSource:      dynacache.GetOrCreatePartition[uint64, *resources.StaleValue[[]byte]](d.MemCache, "/cont/src", dynacache.OptionsPartition{Weight: 70, ClearWhen: dynacache.ClearOnChange}),
507			translationKeyPages:     hmaps.NewSliceCache[page.Page](),
508			currentSite:             first[0],
509			skipRebuildForFilenames: make(map[string]bool),
510			init:                    &hugoSitesInit{},
511			progressReporter:        &progressReporter{},
512		}
513	
514		// Assemble dependencies to be used in hugo.Deps.
515		var dependencies []*hugo.Dependency
516		var depFromMod func(m modules.Module) *hugo.Dependency
517		depFromMod = func(m modules.Module) *hugo.Dependency {
518			dep := &hugo.Dependency{
519				Path:    m.Path(),
520				Version: m.Version(),
521				Time:    m.Time(),
522				Vendor:  m.Vendor(),
523			}
524	
525			// These are pointers, but this all came from JSON so there's no recursive navigation,
526			// so just create new values.
527			if m.Replace() != nil {
528				dep.Replace = depFromMod(m.Replace())
529			}
530			if m.Owner() != nil {
531				dep.Owner = depFromMod(m.Owner())
532			}
533			return dep
534		}
535		for _, m := range d.Paths.AllModules() {
536			dependencies = append(dependencies, depFromMod(m))
537		}
538	
539		// Create providers that avoid naming conflicts with HugoSites fields.
540		sp := hugoSitesSitesProvider{h: h}
541	
542		opts := page.HugoInfoOptions{
543			Conf:                      h.Configs.GetFirstLanguageConfig(),
544			HugoInfoHugoSitesProvider: sp,
545			Deps:                      dependencies,
546		}
547	
548		if bi := hugo.GetBuildInfo(); bi != nil {
549			opts.CommitHash = bi.Revision
550			opts.BuildDate = bi.RevisionTime
551			opts.GoVersion = bi.GoVersion
552		}
553	
554		if opts.BuildDate == "" {
555			opts.BuildDate = hugo.GetBuildDate()
556		}
557	
558		h.hugoInfo = page.NewHugoInfo(opts)
559	
560		var prototype *deps.Deps
561	
562		var i int
563		for _, v := range sitesVersionsRoles {
564			for _, r := range v {
565				for _, s := range r {
566					s.h = h
567					// The template store needs to be initialized after the h container is set on s.
568					if i == 0 {
569						templateStore, err := tplimpl.NewStore(
570							tplimpl.StoreOptions{
571								Fs:                     s.BaseFs.Layouts.Fs,
572								Log:                    s.Log,
573								Watching:               s.Conf.Watching(),
574								PathParser:             s.Conf.PathParser(),
575								Metrics:                d.Metrics,
576								OutputFormats:          s.conf.OutputFormats.Config,
577								MediaTypes:             s.conf.MediaTypes.Config,
578								DefaultOutputFormat:    s.conf.DefaultOutputFormat,
579								TaxonomySingularPlural: s.conf.Taxonomies,
580								RenderHooks:            s.conf.Markup.Goldmark.RenderHooks,
581							}, tplimpl.SiteOptions{
582								Site:          s,
583								TemplateFuncs: tplimplinit.CreateFuncMap(s.Deps),
584							})
585						if err != nil {
586							return nil, err
587						}
588						s.Deps.TemplateStore = templateStore
589					} else {
590						s.Deps.TemplateStore = prototype.TemplateStore.WithSiteOpts(
591							tplimpl.SiteOptions{
592								Site:          s,
593								TemplateFuncs: tplimplinit.CreateFuncMap(s.Deps),
594							})
595					}
596					if err := s.Deps.Compile(prototype); err != nil {
597						return nil, err
598					}
599					if i == 0 {
600						prototype = s.Deps
601					}
602	
603					i++
604				}
605			}
606		}
607	
608		h.fatalErrorHandler = &fatalErrorHandler{
609			h:     h,
610			donec: make(chan bool),
611		}
612	
613		h.init.data = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
614			return h.loadData()
615		})
616	
617		h.init.gitInfo = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
618			return h.loadGitInfo()
619		})
620	
621		return h, nil
622	}

624	// GetSiteDimension returns the value of the specified site dimension (such as language, version, or role)
625	// based on the provided dimension name string. If the dimension name is unknown, it panics.
626	func (s *Site) Dimension(d string) page.SiteDimension {
627		switch d {
628		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Language):
629			return s.language
630		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Version):
631			return s.version
632		case sitesmatrix.DimensionName(sitesmatrix.Role):
633			return s.role
634		default:
635			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown dimension %q", d))
636		}
637	}

817		return s.init.taxonomies.Value(context.Background())
818	}
819	
820	type (
821		taxonomiesConfig       map[string]string
822		taxonomiesConfigValues struct {
823			views          []viewName
824			viewsByTreeKey map[string]viewName
825		}
826	)
827	
828	func (t taxonomiesConfig) Values() taxonomiesConfigValues {
829		var views []viewName
830		for k, v := range t {
831			views = append(views, viewName{singular: k, plural: v, pluralTreeKey: cleanTreeKey(v)})
832		}
833		sort.Slice(views, func(i, j int) bool {
834			return views[i].plural < views[j].plural
835		})
836	
837		viewsByTreeKey := make(map[string]viewName)
838		for _, v := range views {
839			viewsByTreeKey[v.pluralTreeKey] = v
840		}
841	
842		return taxonomiesConfigValues{
843			views:          views,
844			viewsByTreeKey: viewsByTreeKey,
845		}
846	}

857	func (init *siteInit) Reset() {

864	func (s *Site) prepareInits() {
865		s.init = &siteInit{}
866	
867		s.init.prevNext = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
868			regularPages := s.RegularPages()
869			if s.conf.Page.NextPrevSortOrder == "asc" {
870				regularPages = regularPages.Reverse()
871			}
872			for i, p := range regularPages {
873				np, ok := p.(nextPrevProvider)
874				if !ok {
875					continue
876				}
877	
878				pos := np.getNextPrev()
879				if pos == nil {
880					continue
881				}
882	
883				pos.nextPage = nil
884				pos.prevPage = nil
885	
886				if i > 0 {
887					pos.nextPage = regularPages[i-1]
888				}
889	
890				if i < len(regularPages)-1 {
891					pos.prevPage = regularPages[i+1]
892				}
893			}
894			return nil
895		})
896	
897		s.init.prevNextInSection = hsync.OnceMoreFunc(func(ctx context.Context) error {
898			setNextPrev := func(pas page.Pages) {
899				for i, p := range pas {
900					np, ok := p.(nextPrevInSectionProvider)
901					if !ok {
902						continue
903					}
904	
905					pos := np.getNextPrevInSection()
906					if pos == nil {
907						continue
908					}
909	
910					pos.nextPage = nil
911					pos.prevPage = nil
912	
913					if i > 0 {
914						pos.nextPage = pas[i-1]
915					}
916	
917					if i < len(pas)-1 {
918						pos.prevPage = pas[i+1]
919					}
920				}
921			}
922	
923			sections := s.pageMap.getPagesInSection(
924				pageMapQueryPagesInSection{
925					pageMapQueryPagesBelowPath: pageMapQueryPagesBelowPath{
926						Path:    "",
927						KeyPart: "sectionorhome",
928						Include: pagePredicates.KindSection.Or(pagePredicates.KindHome).BoolFunc(),
929					},
930					IncludeSelf: true,
931					Recursive:   true,
932				},
933			)
934	
935			for _, section := range sections {
936				ps := section.RegularPages()
937				if s.conf.Page.NextPrevInSectionSortOrder == "asc" {
938					ps = ps.Reverse()
939				}
940				setNextPrev(ps)
941			}
942	
943			return nil
944		})
945	
946		s.init.menus = hsync.OnceMoreValue(func(ctx context.Context) navigation.Menus {
947			m, err := s.assembleMenus()
948			if err != nil {
949				panic(err)
950			}
951			return m
952		})
953	
954		s.init.taxonomies = hsync.OnceMoreValue(func(ctx context.Context) page.TaxonomyList {
955			taxonomies, err := s.pageMap.CreateSiteTaxonomies(ctx)
956			if err != nil {
957				panic(err)
958			}
959			return taxonomies
960		})
961	}
962	
963	func (s *Site) Menus() navigation.Menus {
964		s.CheckReady()
965		return s.init.menus.Value(context.Background())
966	}

968	func (s *Site) initRenderFormats() {
969		formatSet := make(map[string]bool)
970		formats := output.Formats{}
971	
972		w := &doctree.NodeShiftTreeWalker[contentNode]{
973			Tree: s.pageMap.treePages,
974			Handle: func(key string, n contentNode) (radix.WalkFlag, error) {
975				if p, ok := n.(*pageState); ok {
976					for _, f := range p.m.pageConfig.ConfiguredOutputFormats {
977						if !formatSet[f.Name] {
978							formats = append(formats, f)
979							formatSet[f.Name] = true
980						}
981					}
982				}
983				return radix.WalkContinue, nil
984			},
985		}
986	
987		if err := w.Walk(context.TODO()); err != nil {
988			panic(err)
989		}
990	
991		// Add the per kind configured output formats
992		for _, kind := range kinds.AllKindsInPages {
993			if siteFormats, found := s.conf.C.KindOutputFormats[kind]; found {
994				for _, f := range siteFormats {
995					if !formatSet[f.Name] {
996						formats = append(formats, f)
997						formatSet[f.Name] = true
998					}
999				}
1000			}
1001		}
1002	
1003		sort.Sort(formats)
1004		s.renderFormats = formats
1005	}

1023	type siteRefLinker struct {
1024		s *Site
1025	
1026		errorLogger logg.LevelLogger
1027		notFoundURL string
1028	}
1029	
1030	func newSiteRefLinker(s *Site) siteRefLinker {
1031		logger := s.Log.Error()
1032	
1033		notFoundURL := s.conf.RefLinksNotFoundURL
1034		errLevel := s.conf.RefLinksErrorLevel
1035		if strings.EqualFold(errLevel, "warning") {
1036			logger = s.Log.Warn()
1037		}
1038		return siteRefLinker{s: s, errorLogger: logger, notFoundURL: notFoundURL}
1039	}
1040	
1041	func (s siteRefLinker) logNotFound(ref, what string, p page.Page, position text.Position) {
1042		if position.IsValid() {
1043			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q: %s: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, position.String(), what)
1044		} else if p == nil {
1045			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, what)
1046		} else {
1047			s.errorLogger.Logf("[%s] REF_NOT_FOUND: Ref %q from page %q: %s", s.s.Lang(), ref, p.Path(), what)
1048		}
1049	}
1050	
1051	func (s *siteRefLinker) refLink(ref string, source any, relative bool, outputFormat string) (string, error) {
1052		p, err := unwrapPage(source)
1053		if err != nil {
1054			return "", err
1055		}
1056	
1057		var refURL *url.URL
1058	
1059		ref = filepath.ToSlash(ref)
1060	
1061		refURL, err = url.Parse(ref)
1062		if err != nil {
1063			return s.notFoundURL, err
1064		}
1065	
1066		var target page.Page
1067		var link string
1068	
1069		if refURL.Path != "" {
1070			var err error
1071			target, err = s.s.getPageRef(p, refURL.Path)
1072			var pos text.Position
1073			if err != nil || target == nil {
1074				if p, ok := source.(text.Positioner); ok {
1075					pos = p.Position()
1076				}
1077			}
1078	
1079			if err != nil {
1080				s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, err.Error(), p, pos)
1081				return s.notFoundURL, nil
1082			}
1083	
1084			if target == nil {
1085				s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, "page not found", p, pos)
1086				return s.notFoundURL, nil
1087			}
1088	
1089			var permalinker Permalinker = target
1090	
1091			if outputFormat != "" {
1092				o := target.OutputFormats().Get(outputFormat)
1093	
1094				if o.IsZero() {
1095					s.logNotFound(refURL.Path, fmt.Sprintf("output format %q", outputFormat), p, pos)
1096					return s.notFoundURL, nil
1097				}
1098				permalinker = o
1099			}
1100	
1101			if relative {
1102				link = permalinker.RelPermalink()
1103			} else {
1104				link = permalinker.Permalink()
1105			}
1106		}
1107	
1108		if refURL.Fragment != "" {
1109			_ = target
1110			link = link + "#" + refURL.Fragment
1111	
1112			if pctx, ok := target.(pageContext); ok {
1113				if refURL.Path != "" {
1114					if di, ok := pctx.getContentConverter().(converter.DocumentInfo); ok {
1115						link = link + di.AnchorSuffix()
1116					}
1117				}
1118			} else if pctx, ok := p.(pageContext); ok {
1119				if di, ok := pctx.getContentConverter().(converter.DocumentInfo); ok {
1120					link = link + di.AnchorSuffix()
1121				}
1122			}
1123	
1124		}
1125	
1126		return link, nil
1127	}

1133	type WhatChanged struct {
1134		mu sync.Mutex
1135	
1136		needsPagesAssembly bool
1137	
1138		ids map[identity.Identity]bool
1139	}
1140	
1141	func (w *WhatChanged) init() {
1142		if w.ids == nil {
1143			w.ids = make(map[identity.Identity]bool)
1144		}
1145	}
1146	
1147	func (w *WhatChanged) Add(ids ...identity.Identity) {
1148		w.mu.Lock()
1149		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1150	
1151		w.init()
1152	
1153		for _, id := range ids {
1154			w.ids[id] = true
1155		}
1156	}
1157	
1158	func (w *WhatChanged) Clear() {
1159		w.mu.Lock()
1160		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1161		w.clear()
1162	}
1163	
1164	func (w *WhatChanged) clear() {
1165		w.ids = nil
1166	}
1167	
1168	func (w *WhatChanged) Changes() []identity.Identity {
1169		if w == nil || w.ids == nil {
1170			return nil
1171		}
1172		return slices.Collect(maps.Keys(w.ids))
1173	}
1174	
1175	func (w *WhatChanged) Drain() []identity.Identity {
1176		w.mu.Lock()
1177		defer w.mu.Unlock()
1178		ids := w.Changes()
1179		w.clear()
1180		return ids
1181	}

1193	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsFilter(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1194		seen := make(map[fsnotify.Event]bool)
1195	
1196		n := 0
1197		for _, ev := range events {
1198			// Avoid processing the same event twice.
1199			if seen[ev] {
1200				continue
1201			}
1202			seen[ev] = true
1203	
1204			if h.SourceSpec.IgnoreFile(ev.Name) {
1205				continue
1206			}
1207	
1208			if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" { // When a file system is HFS+, its filepath is in NFD form.
1209				ev.Name = norm.NFC.String(ev.Name)
1210			}
1211	
1212			events[n] = ev
1213			n++
1214		}
1215		events = events[:n]
1216	
1217		eventOrdinal := func(e fsnotify.Event) int {
1218			// Pull the structural changes to the top.
1219			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1220				return 1
1221			}
1222			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Remove) {
1223				return 2
1224			}
1225			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1226				return 3
1227			}
1228			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
1229				return 4
1230			}
1231			return 5
1232		}
1233	
1234		sort.Slice(events, func(i, j int) bool {
1235			// First sort by event type.
1236			if eventOrdinal(events[i]) != eventOrdinal(events[j]) {
1237				return eventOrdinal(events[i]) < eventOrdinal(events[j])
1238			}
1239			// Then sort by name.
1240			return events[i].Name < events[j].Name
1241		})
1242	
1243		return events
1244	}
1245	
1246	type fileEventInfo struct {
1247		fsnotify.Event
1248		fi           os.FileInfo
1249		added        bool
1250		removed      bool
1251		isChangedDir bool
1252	}
1253	
1254	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsApplyInfo(events []fsnotify.Event) []fileEventInfo {
1255		var infos []fileEventInfo
1256		for _, ev := range events {
1257			removed := false
1258			added := false
1259	
1260			if ev.Op&fsnotify.Remove == fsnotify.Remove {
1261				removed = true
1262			}
1263	
1264			fi, statErr := h.Fs.Source.Stat(ev.Name)
1265	
1266			// Some editors (Vim) sometimes issue only a Rename operation when writing an existing file
1267			// Sometimes a rename operation means that file has been renamed other times it means
1268			// it's been updated.
1269			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1270				// If the file is still on disk, it's only been updated, if it's not, it's been moved
1271				if statErr != nil {
1272					removed = true
1273				}
1274			}
1275			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1276				added = true
1277			}
1278	
1279			isChangedDir := statErr == nil && fi.IsDir()
1280	
1281			infos = append(infos, fileEventInfo{
1282				Event:        ev,
1283				fi:           fi,
1284				added:        added,
1285				removed:      removed,
1286				isChangedDir: isChangedDir,
1287			})
1288		}
1289	
1290		n := 0
1291	
1292		for _, ev := range infos {
1293			// Remove any directories that's also represented by a file.
1294			keep := true
1295			if ev.isChangedDir {
1296				for _, ev2 := range infos {
1297					if ev2.fi != nil && !ev2.fi.IsDir() && filepath.Dir(ev2.Name) == ev.Name {
1298						keep = false
1299						break
1300					}
1301				}
1302			}
1303			if keep {
1304				infos[n] = ev
1305				n++
1306			}
1307		}
1308		infos = infos[:n]
1309	
1310		return infos
1311	}
1312	
1313	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsTrim(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1314		seen := make(map[string]bool)
1315		n := 0
1316		for _, ev := range events {
1317			if seen[ev.Name] {
1318				continue
1319			}
1320			seen[ev.Name] = true
1321			events[n] = ev
1322			n++
1323		}
1324		return events
1325	}
1326	
1327	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsContentPaths(p []pathChange) []pathChange {
1328		var bundles []pathChange
1329		var dirs []pathChange
1330		var regular []pathChange
1331	
1332		var others []pathChange
1333		for _, p := range p {
1334			if p.isDir {
1335				dirs = append(dirs, p)
1336			} else {
1337				others = append(others, p)
1338			}
1339		}
1340	
1341		// Remove all files below dir.
1342		if len(dirs) > 0 {
1343			n := 0
1344			for _, o := range others {
1345				keep := true
1346				for _, d := range dirs {
1347					if strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), d.p.Path()+"/") {
1348						keep = false
1349						break
1350					}
1351				}
1352				if keep {
1353					others[n] = o
1354					n++
1355				}
1356			}
1357			others = others[:n]
1358		}
1359	
1360		for _, p := range others {
1361			if p.p.IsBundle() {
1362				bundles = append(bundles, p)
1363			} else {
1364				regular = append(regular, p)
1365			}
1366		}
1367	
1368		// Remove any files below leaf bundles.
1369		// Remove any files in the same folder as branch bundles.
1370		var keepers []pathChange
1371	
1372		for _, o := range regular {
1373			keep := true
1374			for _, b := range bundles {
1375				prefix := b.p.Base() + "/"
1376				if b.p.IsLeafBundle() && strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), prefix) {
1377					keep = false
1378					break
1379				} else if b.p.IsBranchBundle() && o.p.Dir() == b.p.Dir() {
1380					keep = false
1381					break
1382				}
1383			}
1384	
1385			if keep {
1386				keepers = append(keepers, o)
1387			}
1388		}
1389	
1390		keepers = append(dirs, keepers...)
1391		keepers = append(bundles, keepers...)
1392	
1393		return keepers
1394	}

1396	// SitemapAbsURL is a convenience method giving the absolute URL to the sitemap.
1397	func (s *Site) SitemapAbsURL() string {
1398		base := ""
1399		if s.Conf.IsMultilingual() || s.Conf.DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir() {
1400			base = s.Language().Lang
1401		}
1402		p := s.AbsURL(base, false)
1403		if !strings.HasSuffix(p, "/") {
1404			p += "/"
1405		}
1406		p += s.conf.Sitemap.Filename
1407		return p
1408	}
1409	
1410	func (s *Site) createNodeMenuEntryURL(in string) string {
1411		if !strings.HasPrefix(in, "/") {
1412			return in
1413		}
1414		// make it match the nodes
1415		menuEntryURL := in
1416		menuEntryURL = s.PathSpec.URLize(menuEntryURL)
1417		if !s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1418			menuEntryURL = paths.AddContextRoot(s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String(), menuEntryURL)
1419		}
1420		return menuEntryURL
1421	}

1423	func (s *Site) assembleMenus() (navigation.Menus, error) {
1424		menus := make(navigation.Menus)
1425	
1426		type twoD struct {
1427			MenuName, EntryName string
1428		}
1429		flat := map[twoD]*navigation.MenuEntry{}
1430		children := map[twoD]navigation.Menu{}
1431	
1432		// add menu entries from config to flat hash
1433		for name, menu := range s.conf.Menus.Config {
1434			for _, x := range menu {
1435				// Shallow copy the menu entry, so we can set the URL and page without modifying the config.
1436				c := *x
1437				me := &c
1438	
1439				if me.PageRef != "" {
1440					// Try to resolve the page.
1441					me.Page, _ = s.getPage(nil, me.PageRef)
1442				}
1443	
1444				// If page is still nill, we must make sure that we have a URL that considers baseURL etc.
1445				if types.IsNil(me.Page) {
1446					me.ConfiguredURL = s.createNodeMenuEntryURL(me.MenuConfig.URL)
1447				} else {
1448					navigation.SetPageValues(me, me.Page)
1449				}
1450	
1451				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1452			}
1453		}
1454	
1455		sectionPagesMenu := s.conf.SectionPagesMenu
1456	
1457		if sectionPagesMenu != "" {
1458			if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1459				if p.Kind() != kinds.KindSection || !p.m.shouldBeCheckedForMenuDefinitions() {
1460					return false, nil
1461				}
1462	
1463				// The section pages menus are attached to the top level section.
1464				id := p.Section()
1465				if id == "" {
1466					id = "/"
1467				}
1468	
1469				if _, ok := flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, id}]; ok {
1470					return false, nil
1471				}
1472				me := navigation.MenuEntry{
1473					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1474						Identifier: id,
1475						Name:       p.LinkTitle(),
1476						Weight:     p.Weight(),
1477					},
1478					Page: p,
1479				}
1480	
1481				navigation.SetPageValues(&me, p)
1482				flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, me.KeyName()}] = &me
1483				return false, nil
1484			}); err != nil {
1485				return nil, err
1486			}
1487		}
1488	
1489		// Add menu entries provided by pages
1490		if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1491			for name, me := range p.pageMenus.menus() {
1492				if _, ok := flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}]; ok {
1493					err := p.wrapError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate menu entry with identifier %q in menu %q", me.KeyName(), name))
1494					s.Log.Warnln(err)
1495					continue
1496				}
1497				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1498			}
1499			return false, nil
1500		}); err != nil {
1501			return nil, err
1502		}
1503	
1504		// Create Children Menus First
1505		for _, e := range flat {
1506			if e.Parent != "" {
1507				children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}] = children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}].Add(e)
1508			}
1509		}
1510	
1511		// Placing Children in Parents (in flat)
1512		for p, childmenu := range children {
1513			_, ok := flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}]
1514			if !ok {
1515				// if parent does not exist, create one without a URL
1516				flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}] = &navigation.MenuEntry{
1517					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1518						Name: p.EntryName,
1519					},
1520				}
1521			}
1522			flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}].Children = childmenu
1523		}
1524	
1525		// Assembling Top Level of Tree
1526		for menu, e := range flat {
1527			if e.Parent == "" {
1528				_, ok := menus[menu.MenuName]
1529				if !ok {
1530					menus[menu.MenuName] = navigation.Menu{}
1531				}
1532				menus[menu.MenuName] = menus[menu.MenuName].Add(e)
1533			}
1534		}
1535	
1536		return menus, nil
1537	}
1538	
1539	// get any language code to prefix the target file path with.
1540	func (s *Site) getLanguageTargetPathLang(alwaysInSubDir bool) string {
1541		if s.h.Conf.IsMultihost() {
1542			return s.Language().Lang
1543		}
1544	
1545		return s.getLanguagePermalinkLang(alwaysInSubDir)
1546	}
1547	
1548	// get any language code to prefix the relative permalink with.
1549	func (s *Site) getLanguagePermalinkLang(alwaysInSubDir bool) string {
1550		if s.h.Conf.IsMultihost() {
1551			return ""
1552		}
1553	
1554		if s.h.Conf.IsMultilingual() && alwaysInSubDir {
1555			return s.Language().Lang
1556		}
1557	
1558		return s.GetLanguagePrefix()
1559	}
1560	
1561	func (s *Site) getPrefixRole() string {
1562		role := s.roleInternal
1563		if role.Default {
1564			if s.conf.DefaultContentRoleInSubdir {
1565				return role.Name
1566			}
1567			return ""
1568		}
1569		return role.Name
1570	}
1571	
1572	func (s *Site) getPrefixVersion() string {
1573		version := s.versionInternal
1574		if version.Default {
1575			if s.conf.DefaultContentVersionInSubdir {
1576				return version.Name
1577			}
1578			return ""
1579		}
1580		return version.Name
1581	}
1582	
1583	// Prepare site for a new full build.
1584	func (s *Site) resetBuildState(sourceChanged bool) {
1585		s.relatedDocsHandler = s.relatedDocsHandler.Clone()
1586		s.init.Reset()
1587		s.pageMap.Reset()
1588	}
1589	
1590	func (s *Site) errorCollator(results <-chan error, errs chan<- error) {
1591		var errors []error
1592		defer func() {
1593			errs <- s.h.filterAndJoinErrors(errors)
1594			close(errs)
1595		}()
1596		const maxErrors = 10
1597		for e := range results {
1598			errors = append(errors, e)
1599			if len(errors) >= maxErrors {
1600				s.h.Stop()
1601				break
1602			}
1603		}
1604	}
1605	
1606	// GetPage looks up a page of a given type for the given ref.
1607	// In Hugo <= 0.44 you had to add Page Kind (section, home) etc. as the first
1608	// argument and then either a unix styled path (with or without a leading slash))
1609	// or path elements separated.
1610	// When we now remove the Kind from this API, we need to make the transition as painless
1611	// as possible for existing sites. Most sites will use {{ .Site.GetPage "section" "my/section" }},
1612	// i.e. 2 arguments, so we test for that.
1613	func (s *Site) GetPage(ref ...string) (page.Page, error) {
1614		s.CheckReady()
1615		p, err := s.getPageForRefs(ref...)
1616	
1617		if p == nil {
1618			// The nil struct has meaning in some situations, mostly to avoid breaking
1619			// existing sites doing $nilpage.IsDescendant($p), which will always return
1620			// false.
1621			p = page.NilPage
1622		}
1623	
1624		return p, err
1625	}
1626	
1627	func (s *Site) absURLPath(targetPath string) string {
1628		var path string
1629		if s.conf.RelativeURLs {
1630			path = helpers.GetDottedRelativePath(targetPath)
1631		} else {
1632			url := s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String()
1633			if !strings.HasSuffix(url, "/") {
1634				url += "/"
1635			}
1636			path = url
1637		}
1638	
1639		return path
1640	}
1641	
1642	const (
1643		pageDependencyScopeDefault int = iota
1644		pageDependencyScopeGlobal
1645	)
1646	
1647	func (s *Site) renderAndWritePage(statCounter *uint64, targetPath string, p *pageState, d any, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) error {
1648		s.h.onPageRender()
1649		renderBuffer := bp.GetBuffer()
1650		defer bp.PutBuffer(renderBuffer)
1651	
1652		of := p.outputFormat()
1653		p.incrRenderState()
1654	
1655		ctx := s.TemplateStore.PrepareTopLevelRenderCtx(context.Background(), p)
1656		ctx = tpl.Context.DependencyManagerScopedProvider.Set(ctx, p)
1657	
1658		if err := s.renderForTemplate(ctx, p.Kind(), of.Name, d, renderBuffer, templ); err != nil {
1659			return err
1660		}
1661	
1662		if renderBuffer.Len() == 0 {
1663			return nil
1664		}
1665	
1666		isHTML := of.IsHTML
1667		isRSS := of.Name == "rss"
1668	
1669		pd := publisher.Descriptor{
1670			Src:          renderBuffer,
1671			TargetPath:   targetPath,
1672			StatCounter:  statCounter,
1673			OutputFormat: p.outputFormat(),
1674		}
1675	
1676		if isRSS {
1677			// Always canonify URLs in RSS
1678			pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1679		} else if isHTML {
1680			if s.conf.RelativeURLs || s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1681				pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1682			}
1683	
1684			if s.watching() && s.conf.Internal.Running && !s.conf.DisableLiveReload {
1685				pd.LiveReloadBaseURL = s.Conf.BaseURLLiveReload().URL()
1686			}
1687	
1688			// For performance reasons we only inject the Hugo generator tag on the home page.
1689			if p.IsHome() {
1690				pd.AddHugoGeneratorTag = !s.conf.DisableHugoGeneratorInject
1691			}
1692	
1693		}
1694	
1695		return s.publisher.Publish(pd)
1696	}
1697	
1698	var infoOnMissingLayout = map[string]bool{
1699		// The 404 layout is very much optional in Hugo, but we do look for it.
1700		"404": true,
1701	}
1702	
1703	// hookRendererTemplate is the canonical implementation of all hooks.ITEMRenderer,
1704	// where ITEM is the thing being hooked.
1705	type hookRendererTemplate struct {
1706		templateHandler *tplimpl.TemplateStore
1707		templ           *tplimpl.TemplInfo
1708		resolvePosition func(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position
1709	}
1710	
1711	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderLink(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.LinkContext) error {
1712		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1713	}
1714	
1715	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderHeading(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.HeadingContext) error {
1716		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1717	}
1718	
1719	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderCodeblock(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.CodeblockContext) error {
1720		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1721	}
1722	
1723	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderPassthrough(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.PassthroughContext) error {
1724		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1725	}
1726	
1727	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderBlockquote(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.BlockquoteContext) error {
1728		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1729	}
1730	
1731	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderTable(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.TableContext) error {
1732		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1733	}
1734	
1735	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) ResolvePosition(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position {
1736		return hr.resolvePosition(renderContext, pos)
1737	}
1738	
1739	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) IsDefaultCodeBlockRenderer() bool {
1740		return false
1741	}
1742	
1743	func (s *Site) renderForTemplate(ctx context.Context, name, outputFormat string, d any, w io.Writer, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) (err error) {
1744		if templ == nil {
1745			s.logMissingLayout(name, "", "", outputFormat)
1746			return nil
1747		}
1748	
1749		if ctx == nil {
1750			panic("nil context")
1751		}
1752	
1753		if err = s.GetTemplateStore().ExecuteWithContext(ctx, templ, w, d); err != nil {
1754			filename := name
1755			if p, ok := d.(*pageState); ok {
1756				filename = p.String()
1757			}
1758			return fmt.Errorf("render of %q failed: %w", filename, err)
1759		}
1760		return
1761	}
1762	
1763	func (s *Site) shouldBuild(p page.Page) bool {
1764		if !s.conf.IsKindEnabled(p.Kind()) {
1765			return false
1766		}
1767		return shouldBuild(s.Conf.BuildFuture(), s.Conf.BuildExpired(),
1768			s.Conf.BuildDrafts(), p.Draft(), p.PublishDate(), p.ExpiryDate())
1769	}
1770	
1771	func shouldBuild(buildFuture bool, buildExpired bool, buildDrafts bool, Draft bool,
1772		publishDate time.Time, expiryDate time.Time,
1773	) bool {
1774		if !(buildDrafts || !Draft) {
1775			return false
1776		}
1777		hnow := htime.Now()
1778		if !buildFuture && !publishDate.IsZero() && publishDate.After(hnow) {
1779			return false
1780		}
1781		if !buildExpired && !expiryDate.IsZero() && expiryDate.Before(hnow) {
1782			return false
1783		}
1784		return true
1785	}
1786	
1787	func (s *Site) render(ctx *siteRenderContext) (err error) {
1788		if err := page.Clear(); err != nil {
1789			return err
1790		}
1791	
1792		if err = s.renderPages(ctx); err != nil {
1793			return
1794		}
1795	
1796		if !ctx.shouldRenderStandalonePage("") {
1797			return
1798		}
1799	
1800		return
1801	}
1802	
1803	func (s *Site) String() string {
1804		if s == nil {
1805			return "Site (nil)"
1806		}
1807	
1808		return fmt.Sprintf("Site %v: %s", s.siteVector, s.resolveDimensionNames())
1809	}
1810

14	package tplimpl
15	
16	// Increments on breaking changes.
17	const TemplateVersion = 2
18	
19	// ParseInfo holds information about a parsed ntemplate.
20	type ParseInfo struct {
21		// Set for shortcode templates with any {{ .Inner }}
22		IsInner bool
23	
24		// Set for partial templates with any {{ inner }} or {{ templates.Inner }}
25		HasPartialInner bool
26	
27		// Set for partials with a return statement.
28		HasReturn bool
29	
30		// Config extracted from template.
31		Config ParseConfig
32	}
33	
34	func (info ParseInfo) IsZero() bool {
35		return info.Config.Version == 0
36	}
37	
38	// ParseConfig holds configuration extracted from the template.
39	type ParseConfig struct {
40		Version int
41	}
42	
43	var defaultParseConfig = ParseConfig{
44		Version: TemplateVersion,
45	}
46	
47	var defaultParseInfo = ParseInfo{
48		Config: defaultParseConfig,
49	}

14	package converter
15	
16	import (
17		"bytes"
18		"context"
19	
20		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hexec"
21		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/loggers"
22		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config"
23		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/identity"
24		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/converter/hooks"
25		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/highlight"
26		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/markup_config"
27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/tableofcontents"
28		"github.com/spf13/afero"
29	)
30	
31	// ProviderConfig configures a new Provider.
32	type ProviderConfig struct {
33		Conf      config.AllProvider // Project config
34		ContentFs afero.Fs
35		Logger    loggers.Logger
36		Exec      *hexec.Exec
37		highlight.Highlighter
38	}
39	
40	func (p ProviderConfig) MarkupConfig() markup_config.Config {
41		return p.Conf.GetConfigSection("markup").(markup_config.Config)
42	}
43	
44	// ProviderProvider creates converter providers.
45	type ProviderProvider interface {
46		New(cfg ProviderConfig) (Provider, error)
47	}
48	
49	// Provider creates converters.
50	type Provider interface {
51		New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
52		Name() string
53	}
54	
55	// NewProvider creates a new Provider with the given name.
56	func NewProvider(name string, create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)) Provider {
57		return newConverter{
58			name:   name,
59			create: create,
60		}
61	}
62	
63	type newConverter struct {
64		name   string
65		create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
66	}
67	
68	func (n newConverter) New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error) {
69		return n.create(ctx)
70	}
71	
72	func (n newConverter) Name() string {
73		return n.name
74	}
75	
76	var NopConverter = new(nopConverter)
77	
78	type nopConverter int
79	
80	func (nopConverter) Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error) {
81		return &bytes.Buffer{}, nil
82	}
83	
84	func (nopConverter) Supports(feature identity.Identity) bool {
85		return false
86	}
87	
88	// Converter wraps the Convert method that converts some markup into
89	// another format, e.g. Markdown to HTML.
90	type Converter interface {
91		Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error)
92	}
93	
94	// ParseRenderer is an optional interface.
95	// The Goldmark converter implements this, and this allows us
96	// to extract the ToC without having to render the content.
97	type ParseRenderer interface {
98		Parse(RenderContext) (ResultParse, error)
99		Render(RenderContext, any) (ResultRender, error)
100	}
101	
102	// ResultRender represents the minimum returned from Convert and Render.
103	type ResultRender interface {
104		Bytes() []byte
105	}
106	
107	// ResultParse represents the minimum returned from Parse.
108	type ResultParse interface {
109		Doc() any
110		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
111	}
112	
113	// DocumentInfo holds additional information provided by some converters.
114	type DocumentInfo interface {
115		AnchorSuffix() string
116	}
117	
118	// TableOfContentsProvider provides the content as a ToC structure.
119	type TableOfContentsProvider interface {
120		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
121	}
122	
123	// AnchorNameSanitizer tells how a converter sanitizes anchor names.
124	type AnchorNameSanitizer interface {
125		SanitizeAnchorName(s string) string
126	}
127	
128	// Bytes holds a byte slice and implements the Result interface.
129	type Bytes []byte
130	
131	// Bytes returns itself
132	func (b Bytes) Bytes() []byte {
133		return b
134	}
135	
136	// DocumentContext holds contextual information about the document to convert.
137	type DocumentContext struct {
138		Document       any              // May be nil. Usually a page.Page
139		DocumentLookup func(uint64) any // May be nil.
140		DocumentID     string
141		DocumentName   string
142		Filename       string
143	}
144	
145	// RenderContext holds contextual information about the content to render.
146	type RenderContext struct {
147		// Ctx is the context.Context for the current Page render.
148		Ctx context.Context
149	
150		// Src is the content to render.
151		Src []byte
152	
153		// SourceInfo holds optional information about the source of the content to render.
154		SourceInfo any
155	
156		// Whether to render TableOfContents.
157		RenderTOC bool
158	
159		// GerRenderer provides hook renderers on demand.
160		GetRenderer hooks.GetRendererFunc
161	}
162

200	type shortcode struct {
201		name      string
202		isInline  bool  // inline shortcode. Any inner will be a Go template.
203		isClosing bool  // whether a closing tag was provided
204		inner     []any // string or nested shortcode
205		params    any   // map or array
206		ordinal   int
207	
208		indentation string // indentation from source.
209	
210		templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo
211	
212		// If set, the rendered shortcode is sent as part of the surrounding content
213		// to Goldmark and similar.
214		// Before Hug0 0.55 we didn't send any shortcode output to the markup
215		// renderer, and this flag told Hugo to process the {{ .Inner }} content
216		// separately.
217		// The old behavior can be had by starting your shortcode template with:
218		//    {{ $_hugo_config := `{ "version": 1 }`}}
219		doMarkup bool
220	
221		// the placeholder in the source when passed to Goldmark etc.
222		// This also identifies the rendered shortcode.
223		placeholder string
224	
225		pos    int // the position in bytes in the source file
226		length int // the length in bytes in the source file
227	}
228	
229	func (s shortcode) insertPlaceholder() bool {
230		return !s.doMarkup || s.configVersion() == 1
231	}
232	
233	func (s shortcode) needsInner() bool {
234		return s.templ != nil && s.templ.ParseInfo.IsInner
235	}
236	
237	func (s shortcode) configVersion() int {
238		if s.templ == nil {
239			// Not set for inline shortcodes.
240			return 2
241		}
242		return s.templ.ParseInfo.Config.Version
243	}
244	
245	func (s shortcode) innerString() string {
246		var sb strings.Builder
247	
248		for _, inner := range s.inner {
249			sb.WriteString(inner.(string))
250		}
251	
252		return sb.String()
253	}
254	
255	func (sc shortcode) String() string {
256		// for testing (mostly), so any change here will break tests!
257		var params any
258		switch v := sc.params.(type) {
259		case map[string]any:
260			// sort the keys so test assertions won't fail
261			var keys []string
262			for k := range v {
263				keys = append(keys, k)
264			}
265			sort.Strings(keys)
266			tmp := make(map[string]any)
267	
268			for _, k := range keys {
269				tmp[k] = v[k]
270			}
271			params = tmp
272	
273		default:
274			// use it as is
275			params = sc.params
276		}
277	
278		return fmt.Sprintf("%s(%q, %t){%s}", sc.name, params, sc.doMarkup, sc.inner)
279	}
280	
281	// shared between all pages from the same source file.
282	type shortcodeParseInfo struct {
283		filename           string
284		firstTemplateStore *tplimpl.TemplateStore // This represents the first site and must not be used for execution.
285	
286		// Ordered list of shortcodes.
287		shortcodes []*shortcode
288	
289		// All the shortcode names in this set.
290		nameSet *maphelpers.ConcurrentSet[string]
291	
292		// Configuration
293		enableInlineShortcodes bool
294	}
295	
296	func (s *shortcodeParseInfo) addName(name string) {
297		s.nameSet.Add(name)
298	}

14	package goldmark
15	
16	import (
17		"bytes"
18		"regexp"
19		"strings"
20	
21		"github.com/microcosm-cc/bluemonday"
22		strikethroughAst "github.com/yuin/goldmark/extension/ast"
23	
24		emojiAst "github.com/yuin/goldmark-emoji/ast"
25	
26		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo-goldmark-extensions/extras"
27		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo-goldmark-extensions/passthrough"
28		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/tableofcontents"
29	
30		"github.com/yuin/goldmark"
31		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/ast"
32		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/parser"
33		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/renderer"
34		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/text"
35		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/util"
36	)
37	
38	var (
39		tocResultKey = parser.NewContextKey()
40		tocEnableKey = parser.NewContextKey()
41	)
42	
43	type tocTransformer struct {
44		r renderer.Renderer
45	}
46	
47	func (t *tocTransformer) Transform(n *ast.Document, reader text.Reader, pc parser.Context) {
48		if b, ok := pc.Get(tocEnableKey).(bool); !ok || !b {
49			return
50		}
51	
52		var (
53			toc         tableofcontents.Builder
54			tocHeading  = &tableofcontents.Heading{}
55			level       int
56			row         = -1
57			inHeading   bool
58			headingText bytes.Buffer
59		)
60	
61		if ids := pc.IDs().(stringValuesProvider).StringValues(); len(ids) > 0 {
62			toc.SetIdentifiers(ids)
63		}
64	
65		ast.Walk(n, func(n ast.Node, entering bool) (ast.WalkStatus, error) {
66			s := ast.WalkStatus(ast.WalkContinue)
67			if n.Kind() == ast.KindHeading {
68				if inHeading && !entering {
69					tocHeading.Title = sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle(headingText.String())
70					headingText.Reset()
71					toc.AddAt(tocHeading, row, level-1)
72					tocHeading = &tableofcontents.Heading{}
73					inHeading = false
74					return s, nil
75				}
76	
77				inHeading = true
78			}
79	
80			if !(inHeading && entering) {
81				return s, nil
82			}
83	
84			switch n.Kind() {
85			case ast.KindHeading:
86				heading := n.(*ast.Heading)
87				level = heading.Level
88	
89				if level == 1 || row == -1 {
90					row++
91				}
92	
93				id, found := heading.AttributeString("id")
94				if found {
95					tocHeading.ID = string(id.([]byte))
96					tocHeading.Level = level
97				}
98			case
99				ast.KindCodeSpan,
100				ast.KindLink,
101				ast.KindImage,
102				ast.KindEmphasis,
103				strikethroughAst.KindStrikethrough,
104				emojiAst.KindEmoji,
105				extras.KindDelete,
106				extras.KindInsert,
107				extras.KindMark,
108				extras.KindSubscript,
109				extras.KindSuperscript:
110				err := t.r.Render(&headingText, reader.Source(), n)
111				if err != nil {
112					return s, err
113				}
114	
115				return ast.WalkSkipChildren, nil
116			case
117				ast.KindAutoLink,
118				ast.KindRawHTML,
119				ast.KindText,
120				ast.KindString,
121				passthrough.KindPassthroughInline,
122				passthrough.KindPassthroughBlock:
123				err := t.r.Render(&headingText, reader.Source(), n)
124				if err != nil {
125					return s, err
126				}
127			}
128	
129			return s, nil
130		})
131	
132		pc.Set(tocResultKey, toc.Build())
133	}
134	
135	type tocExtension struct {
136		options []renderer.Option
137	}
138	
139	func newTocExtension(options []renderer.Option) goldmark.Extender {
140		return &tocExtension{
141			options: options,
142		}
143	}
144	
145	func (e *tocExtension) Extend(m goldmark.Markdown) {
146		r := goldmark.DefaultRenderer()
147		r.AddOptions(e.options...)
148		m.Parser().AddOptions(parser.WithASTTransformers(util.Prioritized(&tocTransformer{
149			r: r,
150		},
151			// This must run after the ID generation (priority 100).
152			110)))
153	}
154	
155	var tocSanitizerPolicy = newTOCSanitizerPolicy()
156	
157	// newTOCSanitizerPolicy returns a bluemonday policy for sanitizing TOC heading
158	// titles against an allowlist of inline HTML elements and attributes,
159	// specifically excluding anchor elements to prevent links within TOC heading
160	// titles.
161	func newTOCSanitizerPolicy() *bluemonday.Policy {
162		p := bluemonday.NewPolicy()
163		p.AllowElements(
164			"abbr", "b", "bdi", "bdo", "br", "cite", "code", "data", "del", "dfn",
165			"em", "i", "ins", "kbd", "mark", "q", "rp", "rt", "ruby", "s", "samp",
166			"small", "span", "strong", "sub", "sup", "time", "u", "var", "wbr",
167		)
168		p.AllowStandardAttributes()
169		p.AllowStyling()
170		p.AllowImages()
171		p.AllowAttrs("cite").OnElements("del", "ins", "q")
172		p.AllowAttrs("datetime").OnElements("del", "ins", "time")
173		p.AllowAttrs("value").OnElements("data")
174		return p
175	}
176	
177	// tocPassthroughRenderer renders passthrough nodes as raw text for the TOC.
178	type tocPassthroughRenderer struct{}
179	
180	func (r *tocPassthroughRenderer) RegisterFuncs(reg renderer.NodeRendererFuncRegisterer) {
181		reg.Register(passthrough.KindPassthroughInline, r.render)
182		reg.Register(passthrough.KindPassthroughBlock, r.render)
183	}
184	
185	func (r *tocPassthroughRenderer) render(w util.BufWriter, src []byte, node ast.Node, entering bool) (ast.WalkStatus, error) {
186		if !entering {
187			return ast.WalkContinue, nil
188		}
189		switch nn := node.(type) {
190		case *passthrough.PassthroughInline:
191			w.Write(nn.Segment.Value(src))
192		case *passthrough.PassthroughBlock:
193			for i := range nn.Lines().Len() {
194				line := nn.Lines().At(i)
195				w.Write(line.Value(src))
196			}
197		}
198		return ast.WalkSkipChildren, nil
199	}
200	
201	var whiteSpaceRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
202	
203	// sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle sanitizes s for use as a TOC heading title.
204	func sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle(s string) string {
205		if strings.IndexByte(s, '<') == -1 {
206			return s
207		}
208	
209		// Sanitize the string.
210		ss := tocSanitizerPolicy.Sanitize(s)
211	
212		// Remove extraneous whitespace.
213		return whiteSpaceRe.ReplaceAllString(strings.TrimSpace(ss), " ")
214	}

27	
28	type AttributesProvider interface {
29		// Attributes passed in from Markdown (e.g. { attrName1=attrValue1 attrName2="attr Value 2" }).
30		Attributes() map[string]any
31	}
32	
33	// LinkContext is the context passed to a link render hook.
34	type LinkContext interface {
35		PageProvider
36	
37		// The link URL.
38		Destination() string
39	
40		// The link title attribute.
41		Title() string
42	
43		// The rendered (HTML) text.
44		Text() hstring.HTML
45	
46		// The plain variant of Text.
47		PlainText() string
48	}
49	
50	// ImageLinkContext is the context passed to a image link render hook.
51	type ImageLinkContext interface {
52		LinkContext
53	
54		// Returns true if this is a standalone image and the config option
55		// markup.goldmark.parser.wrapStandAloneImageWithinParagraph is disabled.
56		IsBlock() bool
57	
58		// Zero-based ordinal for all the images in the current document.
59		Ordinal() int
60	}

63	type CodeblockContext interface {
64		BaseContext

66	
67		// Chroma highlighting processing options. This will only be filled if Type is a known Chroma Lexer.
68		Options() map[string]any
69	
70		// The type of code block. This will be the programming language, e.g. bash, when doing code highlighting.
71		Type() string
72	
73		// The text between the code fences.
74		Inner() string
75	}

78	type TableContext interface {

82		THead() []TableRow

84	}

92		Ordinal() int
93	}

95	// BlockquoteContext is the context passed to a blockquote render hook.
96	type BlockquoteContext interface {
97		BaseContext
98	
99		AttributesProvider
100	
101		// The blockquote text.
102		// If type is "alert", this will be the alert text.
103		Text() hstring.HTML
104	
105		/// Returns the blockquote type, one of "regular" and "alert".
106		// Type "alert" indicates that this is a GitHub type alert.
107		Type() string
108	
109		// The GitHub alert type converted to lowercase, e.g. "note".
110		// Only set if Type is "alert".
111		AlertType() string
112	
113		// The alert title.
114		// Currently only relevant for Obsidian alerts.
115		// GitHub does not suport alert titles and will not render alerts with titles.
116		AlertTitle() hstring.HTML
117	
118		// The alert sign,  "+" or "-" or "" used to indicate folding.
119		// Currently only relevant for Obsidian alerts.
120		// GitHub does not suport alert signs and will not render alerts with signs.
121		AlertSign() string
122	}
123	
124	// PassThroughContext is the context passed to a passthrough render hook.
125	type PassthroughContext interface {
126		BaseContext
127		AttributesProvider
128	
129		// Currently one of "inline" or "block".
130		Type() string
131	
132		// The inner content of the passthrough element, excluding the delimiters.
133		Inner() string
134	}
135	
136	type AttributesOptionsSliceProvider interface {
137		AttributesSlice() []attributes.Attribute
138		OptionsSlice() []attributes.Attribute
139	}
140	
141	type LinkRenderer interface {
142		RenderLink(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx LinkContext) error
143	}
144	
145	type CodeBlockRenderer interface {
146		RenderCodeblock(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx CodeblockContext) error
147	}
148	
149	type BlockquoteRenderer interface {
150		RenderBlockquote(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx BlockquoteContext) error
151	}
152	
153	type TableRenderer interface {
154		RenderTable(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx TableContext) error
155	}
156	
157	type PassthroughRenderer interface {
158		RenderPassthrough(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx PassthroughContext) error
159	}
160	
161	type IsDefaultCodeBlockRendererProvider interface {
162		IsDefaultCodeBlockRenderer() bool
163	}
164	
165	// HeadingContext contains accessors to all attributes that a HeadingRenderer
166	// can use to render a heading.
167	type HeadingContext interface {
168		PageProvider
169		// Level is the level of the header (i.e. 1 for top-level, 2 for sub-level, etc.).
170		Level() int
171		// Anchor is the HTML id assigned to the heading.
172		Anchor() string
173		// Text is the rendered (HTML) heading text, excluding the heading marker.
174		Text() hstring.HTML
175		// PlainText is the unrendered version of Text.
176		PlainText() string
177	
178		// Attributes (e.g. CSS classes)
179		AttributesProvider
180	}
181	
182	type PageProvider interface {
183		// Page is the page being rendered.
184		Page() any
185	
186		// PageInner may be different than Page when .RenderShortcodes is in play.
187		// The main use case for this is to include other pages' markdown into the current page
188		// but resolve resources and pages relative to the original.
189		PageInner() any
190	}
191	
192	// HeadingRenderer describes a uniquely identifiable rendering hook.
193	type HeadingRenderer interface {
194		// RenderHeading writes the rendered content to w using the data in w.
195		RenderHeading(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx HeadingContext) error
196	}
197	
198	// ElementPositionResolver provides a way to resolve the start Position
199	// of a markdown element in the original source document.
200	// This may be both slow and approximate, so should only be
201	// used for error logging.
202	type ElementPositionResolver interface {
203		// ResolvePosition returns the position of the element in the original source document, -1 if it's not defined.
204		ResolvePosition(renderContext any, srcPos int) text.Position
205	}
206	
207	type RendererType int
208	
209	const (
210		LinkRendererType RendererType = iota + 1
211		ImageRendererType
212		HeadingRendererType
213		CodeBlockRendererType
214		PassthroughRendererType
215		BlockquoteRendererType
216		TableRendererType
217	)
218	
219	type GetRendererFunc func(t RendererType, id any) any
220	
221	type TableCell struct {
222		Text      hstring.HTML
223		Alignment string // left, center, or right
224	}
225	
226	type TableRow []TableCell
227	
228	type Table struct {
229		THead []TableRow
230		TBody []TableRow
231	}
