   - `BaseContext` from `render.NewBaseContext(ctx, hr, node, src, ordinal)`
   - `level` - Heading level (1-6)
   - `anchor` - ID attribute value
   - `text`, `plainText` - Rendered and plain text of heading
   - `AttributesHolder` - From node attributes
7. Calls `hr.RenderHeading(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, headingContext)`
8. Falls back to `renderHeadingDefault()` (line 499-514) if no hook

**Context Types Returned to Hooks**:
- `linkContext` (line 50-73) - Implements `hooks.LinkContext`
- `imageLinkContext` (line 75-82) - Extends `linkContext`, adds `IsBlock()`
- `headingContext` (line 84-107) - Implements `hooks.HeadingContext`

---

### 6. RENDER HOOK INTERFACES (Types users implement)

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/converter/hooks/hooks.go`**

- `LinkContext` interface (line 34-48):
  - `PageProvider` - `Page()`, `PageInner()`
  - `Destination()`, `Title()`, `Text()`, `PlainText()`

- `ImageLinkContext` interface (line 51-60):
  - Extends `LinkContext`
  - `IsBlock()`, `Ordinal()`

- `HeadingContext` interface (line 167-180):
  - `PageProvider`
  - `Level()`, `Anchor()`, `Text()`, `PlainText()`
  - `AttributesProvider`

- `LinkRenderer` interface (line 141-143):
  - `RenderLink(context.Context, io.Writer, LinkContext) error`

- `HeadingRenderer` interface (line 193-196):
  - `RenderHeading(context.Context, io.Writer, HeadingContext) error`

- `RendererType` constants (line 207-217):
  - `LinkRendererType`, `ImageRendererType`, `HeadingRendererType`, etc.

- `GetRendererFunc` (line 219) - Signature for getting hooks: `func(RendererType, any) any`

---

### 7. ADDITIONAL RENDER HOOKS - Code Blocks, Blockquotes, Tables, Passthrough

**CODE BLOCKS** - `/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/codeblocks/render.go`
- `htmlRenderer.renderCodeBlock()` (line 59-115):
  1. Gets renderer via `ctx.RenderContext().GetRenderer(hooks.CodeBlockRendererType, lang)`
  2. Extracts language from fence info
  3. Parses attributes from info string (line 139-171)
  4. Builds `codeBlockContext` with language, code, and attributes
  5. Calls `cr.RenderCodeblock(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, cbctx)`

**BLOCKQUOTES** - `/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/blockquotes/blockquotes.go`
- `htmlRenderer.renderBlockquote()` (line 61-125):
  1. Captures rendered blockquote text with `PushPos()` / `PopRenderedString()`
  2. Gets renderer via `ctx.RenderContext().GetRenderer(hooks.BlockquoteRendererType, typ)`
  3. Detects GitHub alert syntax with `resolveBlockQuoteAlert()` (line 175-188)
  4. Builds `blockquoteContext` with type (regular/alert) and alert details
  5. Calls `cr.RenderBlockquote(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, bqctx)`

**TABLES** - `/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/tables/tables.go`
- `htmlRenderer.renderTable()` (line 56-98):
  1. Uses `PushValue()`/`PopValue()` to accumulate table structure
  2. `renderHeaderOrRow()` (line 146-159) creates table rows
  3. `renderCell()` (line 108-144) extracts text and adds to row
  4. Gets renderer via `ctx.RenderContext().GetRenderer(hooks.TableRendererType, nil)`
  5. Builds `tableContext` with THead and TBody
  6. Calls `cr.RenderTable(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, tctx)`

**PASSTHROUGH** - `/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/passthrough/passthrough.go`
- `htmlRenderer.renderPassthroughBlock()` (line 92-149):
  1. Gets renderer via `ctx.RenderContext().GetRenderer(hooks.PassthroughRendererType, typ)`
  2. Extracts inner content and type (inline/block)
  3. Builds `passthroughContext` with type and inner text
  4. Calls `pr.RenderPassthrough(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, pctx)`

---

### 8. TABLE OF CONTENTS EXTRACTION

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/toc.go`**
- `newTocExtension()` (line 139-143) - Creates TOC extender
- `tocTransformer.Transform()` (line 47-133) - Walks AST after parsing
  - Collects headings with IDs
  - Renders inline elements (emphasis, code, strikethrough, etc.) within headings
  - Uses `sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle()` (line 204-214) to clean HTML
  - Builds `tableofcontents.Fragments` via `toc.Build()`
  - Stores result: `pc.Set(tocResultKey, toc.Build())`

---

### 9. ID GENERATION & ANCHOR SANITIZATION

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/autoid.go`**
- `newIDFactory()` (line 99-104) - Creates ID generator
- `idFactory.Generate()` (line 121-147) - Generates unique IDs for headings
  - Calls `sanitizeAnchorNameWithHook()` to normalize
  - Detects duplicates and appends `-1`, `-2`, etc.

- `sanitizeAnchorName()` (line 40-42) - Converts heading text to valid anchor ID
- `sanitizeAnchorNameString()` (line 36-38) - String version
- `sanitizeAnchorNameWithHook()` (line 44-85) - Core implementation
  - Supports GitHub (default), GitHub-ASCII, and Blackfriday ID types
  - Normalizes unicode, removes accents for ASCII mode
  - Keeps alphanumeric and underscores, replaces spaces/hyphens with `-`

**Registered in parser context**: `parser.WithIDs(newIDFactory(idType))` (line 328 in convert.go)

---

### 10. ATTRIBUTES AND INTERNAL EXTENSIONS

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/internal/extensions/attributes/attributes.go`**
- `New()` (line 27-29) - Creates attributes extension
- `attrExtension.Extend()` (line 35-47):
  - Adds block attribute parser (priority 100) if `Attribute.Block` enabled
  - Adds AST transformer (priority 100) for ID generation
- `transformer.Transform()` (line 118-169):
  - Generates auto IDs for headings (if `AutoHeadingID`)
  - Applies block attributes to preceding elements
  - Calls `generateAutoID()` (line 171-188) for each heading

**Hugo Context (for `.RenderShortcodes`)** - `/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/hugocontext/hugocontext.go`
- `New()` (line 35-37) - Creates extension
- `hugoContextParser.Parse()` (line 133-153) - Parses `{{__hugo_ctx pid=123}}`
- `hugoContextRenderer.handleHugoContext()` (line 262-278) - Pushes/pops page ID
- `hugoContextTransformer.Transform()` (line 284-311) - Post-processes AST

---

### 11. HIGHLIGHT/CODE BLOCK HIGHLIGHTING

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/highlight/highlight.go`**
- `Highlighter` interface (line 61-66):
  - `Highlight(code, lang string, opts any) (string, error)`
  - `HighlightCodeBlock(ctx hooks.CodeblockContext, opts any) (HighlightResult, error)`
  - Implements `hooks.CodeBlockRenderer`

- `chromaHighlighter.Highlight()` (line 72-84) - Simple highlighting
- `chromaHighlighter.HighlightCodeBlock()` (line 86-123) - Hook-based highlighting
  - Applies options from context
  - Calls `highlight()` (line 165-253) to invoke Chroma
- `chromaHighlighter.RenderCodeblock()` (line 125-142) - Implements `hooks.CodeBlockRenderer`

- `highlight()` function (line 165-253):
  - Gets Chroma lexer for language
  - Tokenizes code
  - Formats with HTML wrapper
  - Returns byte range for inner (unwrapped) content

**Integrated via**: `codeblocks.New()` extension at line 154 of convert.go when `Highlight.CodeFences` enabled

---

### 12. CONFIGURATION

**`/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/goldmark/goldmark_config/config.go`**
- `Config` struct (line 109-115):
  - `Renderer` - HardWraps, XHTML, Unsafe
  - `Parser` - AutoHeadingID, AutoIDType, WrapStandAloneImageWithinParagraph
  - `Extensions` - Table, Strikethrough, Linkify, TaskList, etc.
  - `RenderHooks` - Image and Link hook configuration

- `Parser` struct (line 284-305):
  - `AutoHeadingID` - Enable heading ID generation
  - `AutoIDType` - "github", "github-ascii", or "blackfriday"
  - `Attribute` - ParserAttribute with Title, Block support

- `RenderHooks` struct (line 128-131):
  - `Image.UseEmbedded` - "auto", "always", "fallback", "never"
  - `Link.UseEmbedded` - Same options

---

### 13. COMPLETE CALL FLOW SUMMARY

```
markup.NewConverterProvider()
  └─ goldmark.Provider.New(cfg)
      └─ &goldmarkConverter{md: newMarkdown(cfg)}

Page Content → converter.Converter.Convert(RenderContext)
  ├─ goldmarkConverter.Parse(RenderContext)
  │   ├─ newParserContext() → parser.Context with IDFactory
  │   ├─ md.Parser().Parse(reader, context)
  │   └─ Returns parserResult{doc, toc}
  │
  └─ goldmarkConverter.Render(RenderContext, doc)
      ├─ Create render.Context with buffer & RenderContextDataHolder
      ├─ md.Renderer().Render(context, src, node)
      │   └─ Walks AST, calls node renderers at priorities
      │       ├─ hookedRenderer (priority 100):
      │       │   ├─ renderLink() / renderImage() → GetRenderer(LinkRendererType)
      │       │   ├─ renderHeading() → GetRenderer(HeadingRendererType)
      │       │   └─ Uses PushPos/PopPos to capture rendered children
      │       ├─ blockquotesExtension (priority 100):
      │       │   └─ renderBlockquote() → GetRenderer(BlockquoteRendererType)
      │       ├─ codeblocks.htmlRenderer (priority 100):
      │       │   └─ renderCodeBlock() → GetRenderer(CodeBlockRendererType, lang)
      │       ├─ tables.htmlRenderer (priority 100):
      │       │   └─ renderTable() → GetRenderer(TableRendererType)
      │       ├─ passthrough.htmlRenderer (priority 90):
      │       │   └─ renderPassthroughBlock() → GetRenderer(PassthroughRendererType)
      │       ├─ hugocontext.hugoContextRenderer (priority 50):
      │       │   └─ handleHugoContext() → Manages PushPid/PopPid stack
      │       ├─ TOC transformer (priority 110):
      │       │   └─ Builds TableOfContents.Fragments from headings
      │       └─ Default Goldmark renderers for other nodes
      └─ Returns renderResult{bytes}

HTML + TableOfContents → Page.Content
```

---

### 14. FILE STRUCTURE COMPLETE LISTING

```
/home/bench/repos/hugo/markup/
├── markup.go                                    # ConverterProvider registry
├── converter/
│   ├── converter.go                             # ProviderConfig, DocumentContext, RenderContext, Converter interface
│   └── hooks/hooks.go                           # Hook interfaces (LinkContext, HeadingContext, etc.)
├── goldmark/
│   ├── convert.go                               # Main converter (Parse, Render, Convert methods)
│   ├── render_hooks.go                          # Link, Image, Heading render hook implementations
│   ├── toc.go                                   # Table of contents extraction
│   ├── autoid.go                                # ID generation & sanitization
│   ├── goldmark_config/config.go                # Goldmark configuration types
│   ├── internal/
│   │   ├── render/context.go                    # render.Context, RenderContextDataHolder
│   │   └── extensions/attributes/attributes.go  # Attribute and ID generation transformer
│   ├── blockquotes/blockquotes.go               # Blockquote render hook extension
│   ├── codeblocks/render.go                     # Code block render hook extension
│   ├── tables/tables.go                         # Table render hook extension
│   ├── passthrough/passthrough.go               # Passthrough content render hook
│   └── hugocontext/hugocontext.go               # Hugo context markers for .RenderShortcodes
└── highlight/highlight.go                       # Code highlighting via Chroma
```

This complete mapping shows how content flows through the conversion pipeline, where hooks intercept rendering, and how the HTML is produced and returned.

62	// Build builds all sites. If filesystem events are provided,
63	// this is considered to be a potential partial rebuild.
64	func (h *HugoSites) Build(config BuildCfg, events ...fsnotify.Event) error {
65		if h.isRebuild() && !h.Conf.Watching() && !h.Conf.Running() {
66			return errors.New("Build called multiple times when not in watch or server mode (typically with hugolib.Test(t, files).Build(); Build() is already called once by Test)")
67		}
68		if !h.isRebuild() && terminal.PrintANSIColors(os.Stdout) {
69			// Don't show progress for fast builds.
70			d := debounce.New(250 * time.Millisecond)
71			d(func() {
72				h.progressReporter.Start()
73				h.reportProgress(func() (state terminal.ProgressState, progress float64) {
74					// We don't know how many files to process below, so use the intermediate state as the first progress.
75					return terminal.ProgressIntermediate, 1.0
76				})
77			})
78			defer d(func() {})
79		}
80	
81		infol := h.Log.InfoCommand("build")
82		defer loggers.TimeTrackf(infol, time.Now(), nil, "")
83		defer func() {
84			h.reportProgress(func() (state terminal.ProgressState, progress float64) {
85				return terminal.ProgressHidden, 1.0
86			})
87			h.BuildState.BuildCounter.Add(1)
88		}()
89	
90		if h.Deps == nil {
91			panic("must have deps")
92		}
93	
94		if !config.NoBuildLock {
95			unlock, err := h.BaseFs.LockBuild()
96			if err != nil {
97				return fmt.Errorf("failed to acquire a build lock: %w", err)
98			}
99			defer unlock()
100		}

52	var (
53		_ page.Page                                = (*pageState)(nil)
54		_ collections.Grouper                      = (*pageState)(nil)
55		_ collections.Slicer                       = (*pageState)(nil)
56		_ identity.DependencyManagerScopedProvider = (*pageState)(nil)
57		_ pageContext                              = (*pageState)(nil)
58	)
59	
60	var (
61		pageTypesProvider = resource.NewResourceTypesProvider(media.Builtin.OctetType, pageResourceType)
62		nopPageOutput     = &pageOutput{
63			pagePerOutputProviders: nopPagePerOutput,
64			MarkupProvider:         page.NopPage,
65			ContentProvider:        page.NopPage,
66		}
67	)
68	
69	// pageContext provides contextual information about this page, for error
70	// logging and similar.
71	type pageContext interface {
72		posOffset(offset int) text.Position
73		wrapError(err error) error
74		getContentConverter() converter.Converter
75	}
76	
77	type pageSiteAdapter struct {
78		p page.Page
79		s *Site
80	}
81	
82	func (pa pageSiteAdapter) GetPage(ref string) (page.Page, error) {
83		p, err := pa.s.getPage(pa.p, ref)
84	
85		if p == nil {
86			// The nil struct has meaning in some situations, mostly to avoid breaking
87			// existing sites doing $nilpage.IsDescendant($p), which will always return
88			// false.
89			p = page.NilPage
90		}
91		return p, err
92	}
93	
94	type pageState struct {
95		// Incremented for each new page created.
96		// Note that this will change between builds for a given Page.
97		pid uint64
98	
99		s *Site
100	
101		// This slice will be of same length as the number of global slice of output
102		// formats (for all sites).
103		pageOutputs []*pageOutput
104	
105		// Used to determine if we can reuse content across output formats.
106		pageOutputTemplateVariationsState atomic.Uint32
107	
108		// This will be shifted out when we start to render a new output format.
109		pageOutputIdx int
110		*pageOutput
111	
112		// Common for all output formats.
113		*pageCommon
114	
115		resource.Staler
116		dependencyManager identity.Manager
117	}
118	
119	// This is not accurate and only used for progress reporting.
120	// We can do better, but this will do for now.
121	func (p *pageState) hasRenderableOutput() bool {
122		for _, po := range p.pageOutputs {
123			if po.render {
124				return true
125			}
126		}
127		return false
128	}
129	
130	func (p *pageState) incrPageOutputTemplateVariation() {
131		p.pageOutputTemplateVariationsState.Add(1)
132	}
133	
134	func (ps *pageState) canReusePageOutputContent() bool {
135		return ps.pageOutputTemplateVariationsState.Load() == 1
136	}
137	
138	func (ps *pageState) IdentifierBase() string {
139		return ps.Path()
140	}
141	
142	func (ps *pageState) GetIdentity() identity.Identity {
143		return ps
144	}
145	
146	func (ps *pageState) ForEeachIdentity(f func(identity.Identity) bool) bool {
147		return f(ps)
148	}
149	
150	func (ps *pageState) GetDependencyManager() identity.Manager {
